task_completion.description: Open the original floorplan.dxf file in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application is open, and the window title or tab shows 'floorplan.dxf'. The drawing area displays an architectural floor plan.

task_completion.description: Create the three required GIS layers with correct colors
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layer List panel in LibreCAD shows three new layers: 'GIS_BOUNDARY' with a red color icon, 'GIS_REFERENCE' with a cyan color icon, and 'GIS_LABELS' with a green color icon.

task_completion.description: Draw a red rectangle on the GIS_BOUNDARY layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red rectangle is visible in the drawing area, starting from the origin (0,0) and extending to (1500,1000).

task_completion.description: Draw a cyan crosshair on the GIS_REFERENCE layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: A cyan cross shape (intersecting horizontal and vertical lines) is visible in the drawing area, centered roughly at (750,500).

task_completion.description: Add three green text annotations on the GIS_LABELS layer
task_completion.visual_evidence: Green text annotations reading 'PARCEL ID: 12-045-0023', 'COORDINATE SYSTEM: NAD83', and 'PREPARED BY: GIS DEPT' are visible in the drawing area, stacked near the top left.

task_completion.description: Save the modified drawing as floorplan_gis.dxf
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible with the filename 'floorplan_gis.dxf', or the LibreCAD window title changes to 'floorplan_gis.dxf'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreCAD graphical user interface to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through LibreCAD menus, toolbars, layer panels, and interacting with the drawing canvas.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts (e.g., Python with ezdxf) to bypass the GUI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows running Python scripts or text editors with DXF manipulation code are visible.

integrity.description: The modifications are genuinely drawn within the LibreCAD canvas
integrity.visual_evidence: The new elements (rectangle, crosshair, text) appear integrated into the LibreCAD drawing area and respond to LibreCAD's view controls (like zooming or panning), rather than being overlaid images from another application.
